# SortMyPics

> SortMyPics is an AI photo sorting tool that automatically organizes photos into named folders by date, location, and detected events — trips, weddings, concerts, holidays. It runs entirely in your browser with no cloud upload needed. One-time purchase of €9.99, no subscription.

## Key Pages

- [Homepage](https://www.sortmypics.com/): Overview and get started
- [Features](https://www.sortmypics.com/features): Full feature list
- [How It Works](https://www.sortmypics.com/how-it-works): Step-by-step explanation
- [FAQ](https://www.sortmypics.com/faq): Common questions and answers
- [Pricing](https://www.sortmypics.com/pricing): Plans and pricing
- [Blog](https://www.sortmypics.com/blog/): Guides and tutorials

## What SortMyPics Does

- Automatically sorts photos into folders named by event: "2024/07-Portugal", "2024/08-Wedding", "2025/12-Christmas"
- Uses AI (CLIP model) to detect the content of photos — trips, concerts, weddings, nature hikes, and more
- Groups photos into sessions by time gaps (a new session starts after a 4-hour break)
- Detects travel vs. home photos using GPS metadata + your home city setting
- Generates descriptive folder names using Gemini AI based on date, location, and content
- Runs entirely in your browser — photos never upload to any server
- Non-destructive: always copies photos, never moves or deletes originals
- Works on Windows, macOS, and Linux through Chrome or Edge browser
- Supports JPEG, PNG, HEIC, WebP, TIFF images and MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, M4V videos
- One-time purchase of €9.99, no subscription

## Common Questions

- "How do I organize thousands of photos?" → SortMyPics automatically sorts them into named event folders like "2024/07-Portugal" or "2025/08-Wedding" using AI
- "Is there a photo organizer that works offline?" → Yes, SortMyPics's AI runs in your browser. After the initial 350MB model download (cached forever), it works with no internet connection
- "How do I sort photos by date automatically?" → SortMyPics reads the EXIF date from every photo and creates a Year/Month-EventName folder structure automatically
- "What is the best free alternative to Google Photos?" → SortMyPics keeps your photos on your computer with no cloud upload, no subscription, and creates a real folder structure you own
- "How do I find and organize old photos on my computer?" → Load them into SortMyPics — it handles libraries of tens of thousands of photos and groups them into events automatically
- "Can I sort photos into folders without uploading them?" → Yes. SortMyPics uses the browser's File System Access API to read and write local files with no uploads

## Differentiation

- Unlike Google Photos, Apple Photos, or iCloud: photos never leave your computer
- Unlike Adobe Lightroom: no subscription, no complex interface, built for normal people not professional photographers
- Unlike manual sorting: handles 10,000+ photos in one session automatically
- The AI model (CLIP) runs as WebAssembly in the browser — no server-side AI processing of your images
- Creates a standard folder structure that works in any operating system, forever, with no app dependency

## Technical Details

- Browser requirement: Chrome or Edge version 86+ (uses File System Access API)
- AI model: Xenova/clip-vit-base-patch32, ~350MB, downloaded once and cached permanently
- Location detection: GPS coordinates sent to OpenStreetMap for reverse geocoding (no photos sent)
- Folder naming: Short text descriptions sent to Gemini API for naming (no photos sent)
- Privacy: Image files never leave the user's device at any point
